_meta.title: Duet / RepRapFirmware — state.status (Object Model) → normalized lifecycle
_meta.confidence: 🟡 source-read (docs.duet3d.com + LGPL @duet3d/objectmodel); no Duet on the bench

derived_from.source_note: Clean-room from Duet3D docs + the LGPL @duet3d/objectmodel enum strings. No code copied; the only credential is a user password (never here).

notes: ⚠️ The printing state is `processing` — there is NO value literally named `printing`. (The legacy `rr_status` numeric/letter enum with a literal `printing`/`toolChange` is a different, older, deprecated surface — don't use it.)
There is NO distinct `completed` value — completion is inferred from a job returning to `idle` with the just-finished job's terminal flags set (cancelled/aborted/simulated).
`disconnected` is a client pseudo-status RRF does not emit.
An unlisted value degrades to busy/unknown, never raises.
Normalized vocabulary: idle | printing | paused | busy | connecting | error | offline (+ transient).

mappings.note: THE printing state (not a value named 'printing')
a dry-run for timing — FLAG it; do not count as a physical print
machine-level 'mid tool-change'; carries a toolchange sub-state (state.nextTool/previousTool)
emergency-stopped after M112; recover with M999
client pseudo-status; RRF does not emit it

completion_inference.rule: state.status transitions to `idle` AND the just-finished job carries terminal flags (cancelled / aborted / simulated)

mode_discriminator.comment: which LAN dialect the board is in — confirm in-band, don't guess
mode_discriminator.sbc_dsf: populated (exclusively maintained by DSF in SBC mode); DSF version at sbc.dsf.version (there is NO state.dsfVersion)
